artifact.name: BD_06_INTERNAL_DRAFT_NO_CLIENT_LEAK_REPORT.json
artifact.title: Internal draft, analyst rebuild and no-client-leak gate

ticket_contract.work_type: Internal Draft Workflow / Client Projection Safety
ticket_contract.unique_shape: Fail-closed internal-only ticket; its core acceptance is absence of client leakage, not visible feature richness.
ticket_contract.execution_decision: Separate internal draft model, rebuild action and client projection negative proof.
ticket_contract.overclaim_guard: Analyst-visible drafts must remain invisible to clients until compliance release.

completed_subtasks.name: Internal draft/rebuild state contract
completed_subtasks.summary: Locked the first-class InternalDraft/DraftClassification/UnsupportedClaim/DraftTrace spine, allowed state progression, rebuild requirements and legacy Recommendation-field non-use.

completed_subtasks.name: No-client-leak projection guard
completed_subtasks.summary: Locked negative proof matrix for API, client-route, client projection, export and admin non-bypass leakage cases.

dependency_decisions_applied.effect: Evidence-backed rebuild requires accepted scoped evidence and cannot be substituted by upload, placeholder evidence or generic review.
Internal workbench/task-board affordances may route analysts to draft/rebuild work, but cannot turn queue visibility into client projection, export or release.
Admin/security governance cannot override internal draft clearance, sufficiency, compliance release, export approval or internal payload visibility.

source_evidence.role: Ticket source defining BD-06 split and acceptance evidence.
P0 safety proof for draft, release, export, admin and audit gates.

process_first_result.decision: BD-06 is satisfied as a spec/proof lock over existing implementation; no additional UI was needed.
process_first_result.reason: The ticket's success metric is a confidential draft gate and negative client leakage proof, not screen richness.
process_first_result.internal_process_change: contractualized and proven through JSON artifacts plus existing tests

facts: Internal draft models exist in prisma/schema.prisma.
Production recommendation mutations are tested against overloaded draft-field writes.
P44 Phase 5 tests cover draft creation, classification, unsupported claim, rejection, evidence-backed rebuild, trace map, leakage sweep, advisor handoff and exit certification.
Client projection and export safety tests block internal fields.
assumptions: Historical nullable Recommendation draft fields remain for compatibility, but are not accepted for new draft workflow writes.
Future raw JSON endpoints are outside this proof unless they consume visibilityEngine or the AV27 payload contract.

killed_weak_branches.reason: Would optimize the wrong metric; BD-06 asks for absence of client leakage, not extra UI decoration.

killed_weak_branches.branch: Keep Recommendation fields as normal compatibility fallback
killed_weak_branches.reason: This preserves a leak-prone second truth surface and contradicts process-first traceability.

killed_weak_branches.branch: Rely on admin override for emergency release
killed_weak_branches.reason: Rejected by DG-02; admin governance is non-bypass only.

adversarial_qa.attack: Client role receives an AI draft before compliance release.
Export packages raw internal fields even when UI hides them.
adversarial_qa.expected_defense: export safety rejects forbidden payload classifications and missing redaction controls.

adversarial_qa.attack: Analyst rebuilds after upload but before accepted scoped evidence.
adversarial_qa.expected_defense: rebuildP44DraftWithEvidence throws unless accepted scoped evidence is present.

adversarial_qa.attack: Admin bypasses draft governance or payload visibility.
adversarial_qa.expected_defense: P0 safety/admin non-bypass tests deny force-release, sufficiency, export and internal payload visibility.

measurement_plan: Keep internal draft governance certification green before claiming draft workflow completion.
Run no-leak projection tests whenever adding client routes, export paths or draft fields.
Add a source guard or static scan if future routes expose raw recommendation/draft rows.

ethics_and_fairness.client_protection: No unapproved advice or internal rationale reaches clients.
ethics_and_fairness.advisor_protection: Analysts get rebuild and unsupported-claim feedback without leaking it externally.
ethics_and_fairness.governance_protection: Admin power is restricted to governance/configuration and cannot launder unsafe advice into release.

commands_run.command: pnpm playwright test tests/internal-draft-governance-spine.spec.ts tests/p44-phase5-certification.spec.ts tests/client-visibility-projection.spec.ts tests/true-ux-p0-safety.spec.ts tests/export-safety.spec.ts --workers=1

validation.positive_acceptance_result: Internal draft creation, classification, unsupported-claim handling, rejection, evidence-backed rebuild and advisor-readiness gating are proven.
validation.negative_acceptance_result: Client/export leakage, unclassified handoff, unsupported-claim handoff, rejected progress, upload-only rebuild and admin bypass are blocked.

product_code_delta.reason: Existing implementation already satisfies BD-06 state and no-leak proof requirements. This ticket produced contract/proof artifacts.

ui_delta.reason: No UI changed; screenshot evidence would not prove payload absence.

bold_cleanup_recommendations: Make InternalDraft the only write surface for internal draft content and actively remove new use of Recommendation.clientSummaryDraft / assumptionsJson in workflow code.
Require every future client-facing route, export path and downloadable package to consume the same no-leak projection contract.
Add a hard CI scan for raw InternalDraft, DraftTrace and internal Recommendation fields in app/api client-facing responses before the next broad UI tranche.
Remove or quarantine any historical draft-compatible UI that cannot prove its payload path through visibilityEngine.
remaining_limits: Historical nullable Recommendation draft fields still exist in schema; removing them would be a later migration decision.
BD-06 does not implement new visible workbench controls; BD-05 already identified that as a separate action-board extraction problem.
BD-06 proof is only as complete as the registered typed routes and tests; future ad hoc endpoints need the same guard.
